name: Sierra al Pico Urbión, Picacho del Camperón y Peñas Blancas – trail running circular por el Parque Natural Laguna Negra y Circos Glaciares de Urbión
region_intro: Jogging around Duruelo De La Sierra offers routes through varied terrain, characterized by the glacial cirques and high peaks of the Urbión mountain range. The region features dense pine forests, rocky outcrops, and scenic viewpoints. Running routes often follow natural paths, providing a mix of ascents and descents suitable for trail running. Best jogging routes around Duruelo De La Sierra The most popular jogging route is Sierra al Pico Urbión, Picacho del Camperón y Peñas Blancas, a 5.1 miles (8.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 28 minutes to complete. This challenging route explores the natural park with significant elevation gain. Another top favourite among local runners is Castroviejo and Cueva Serena Trail, a difficult 4.4 miles (7.1 km) path. This route leads through distinctive rock formations and past the Cueva Serena waterfall. Local runners also love the Mirador de Castroviejo, cascada de la Chorla y Cueva Serena, a 9.4 miles (15.1 km) trail leading through pine forests and past viewpoints, often completed in about 1 hour 51 minutes. Jogging around Duruelo De La Sierra is defined by mountain trails, dense pine forests, and dramatic rock formations. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to challenging ascents. The routes in Duruelo De La Sierra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 180 runners have used komoot to explore Duruelo De La Sierra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Duruelo De La Sierra?
A: There are over 20 running routes around Duruelo De La Sierra, offering a variety of options for different skill levels and preferences. The region is known for its dense pine forests and dramatic rock formations.

Q: Are there any easy or moderate running trails for beginners in Duruelo De La Sierra?
A: While many routes in Duruelo De La Sierra are challenging, there are some moderate options available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31432775" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Covaleda&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.3-mile (13.3 km) path with less elevation gain, suitable for those looking for a less strenuous run.

Q: What kind of natural sights can I expect to see along the running routes?
A: The running routes often feature stunning natural landmarks. You can expect to see the high peaks of the Urbión mountain range, dense pine forests, and unique rock formations. Notable attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2516913" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de Urbión&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3328794" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Duero River&lt;/a&gt;, and the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3844856" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Larga&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by waterfalls?
A: Yes, several routes offer views of waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1064167423/castroviejo-and-cueva-serena-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castroviejo and Cueva Serena Trail&lt;/a&gt; specifically leads past the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5414451" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva Serena Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1064168646/mirador-de-castroviejo-cascada-de-la-chorla-y-cueva-serena-trail-running-circular-desde-duruelo-de-la-sierra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de Castroviejo, cascada de la Chorla y Cueva Serena&lt;/a&gt; route, which also features the Cascada de la Chorla.

Q: Are there circular running routes available in Duruelo De La Sierra?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Duruelo De La Sierra are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1064170909/sierra-al-pico-urbion-picacho-del-camperon-y-penas-blancas-trail-running-circular-por-el-parque-natural-laguna-negra-y-circos-glaciares-de-urbion" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra al Pico Urbión, Picacho del Camperón y Peñas Blancas&lt;/a&gt;, which is a circular trail through the Natural Park.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Duruelo De La Sierra?
A: The best time for running in Duruelo De La Sierra is generally from spring through autumn. During these seasons, the weather is more favorable, and the trails are typically clear. Winter can be challenging due to snow and ice, especially at higher elevations in the Urbión mountain range.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the running trails?
A: Absolutely. The mountainous terrain of Duruelo De La Sierra offers numerous scenic viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1064168646/mirador-de-castroviejo-cascada-de-la-chorla-y-cueva-serena-trail-running-circular-desde-duruelo-de-la-sierra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de Castroviejo, cascada de la Chorla y Cueva Serena&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, is named after a prominent viewpoint, offering expansive vistas of the surrounding landscape.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Duruelo De La Sierra?
A: The running routes in Duruelo De La Sierra are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. Runners often praise the challenging ascents, the beauty of the pine forests, and the dramatic rock formations that define the region's trails.

Q: Are there any long-distance running options for experienced trail runners?
A: Yes, for experienced trail runners seeking a significant challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31432902" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Astillero Junction – Covarnantes Rock Formations loop from Almirón&lt;/a&gt; offer a substantial distance of over 16 miles (26 km) with considerable elevation gain, providing a demanding and rewarding experience.

Q: Can I find routes that explore the glacial cirques of Urbión?
A: Yes, the region is characterized by its glacial cirques. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1064170909/sierra-al-pico-urbion-picacho-del-camperon-y-penas-blancas-trail-running-circular-por-el-parque-natural-laguna-negra-y-circos-glaciares-de-urbion" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra al Pico Urbión, Picacho del Camperón y Peñas Blancas&lt;/a&gt; trail is a prime example, taking you through the Natural Park of Laguna Negra and the glacial cirques of Urbión.

Q: What kind of terrain should I expect on the running trails?
A: The terrain in Duruelo De La Sierra is varied, featuring natural paths through dense pine forests, rocky outcrops, and some significant ascents and descents. It's typical trail running terrain, often requiring appropriate footwear and a good level of fitness.
tips: Cresting by the Urbión Peaks, at 2,101 masl we find ourselves in the Picacho del Camperón with magnificent panoramic views of the places that surround us. At the top, a curiosity awaits us, an ice ax nailed to a rock in the style of the Sword of Excalibur.

Pico de Urbión is a mountain located in the Sierra de Urbión, on the border between the provinces of Soria and La Rioja. It is a popular destination for nature lovers and hikers.
The natural beauty of the Sierra de Urbión offers excellent opportunities for photography.
